The genus Sophora is a group of legume plants, commonly known as kowhai in New Zealand. The classification of Sophora macnabiana is further divided into various ranks, including kingdom, family, genus, and species. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other legume species.

The plant's roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds that possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
